lesson.menu_desc: Write professional follow-up emails after the congress -- attachments, replies and staying in touch -- with the present perfect (have/has + past participle; for/since/yet/already/just) -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Professional Emails & Following Up
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 19 -- Professional Emails & Following Up

listenings.text: Hi Marcos, this is David from the Washington congress. I hope you are doing well. I am calling about the email I sent you on Monday. I have attached the updated valuation report and a short draft of our agreement. I have not heard back from you yet, so I just wanted to make sure you received it. The attachment is quite large, so please check that it did not go to your spam folder. If you have already read it, that is great. When you have a moment, please reply and let me know your thoughts. The deal is still pending on our side, but we are ready to move forward as soon as you confirm. Thanks again, and talk soon.

Good morning, Marcos. Here is your email summary for today. You have three messages that need a reply. First, Rachel from the congress has sent the final contract. She is waiting for you to acknowledge it. Second, the supplier has forwarded the new prices, but you have not opened that attachment yet. Third, a new contact named Karen would like to reach out about a future project. She has just added you on LinkedIn. Two replies are still pending from last week, so please follow up today. That is all for now. Have a productive morning.
